description:
Add CallsiteOutcome::Inlined producer to the reducer. On a Stalled
callsite where:
  - callee is in m.fn_by_id (not extern),
  - callee body is is_inlinable (small leaf, not closure target),
  - callee is NOT in boundary_fns,

splice the callee body into the caller at the callsite (mirror of
ir_inline::inline_calls_once or inline_tail_calls_once), mutate the
IR in place, and write Inlined at the CallsiteId. Outcome transitions
Stalled → Inlined.

For now: copy ir_inline's is_inlinable + alpha_rename + absorb_callee
helpers into ir_reducer. Don't delete from ir_inline yet — parallel
operation.

## Acceptance

- An new unit test: a small fn becomes Inlined when reducer can't
  fold its arguments.
- All 842 tests green.
- Goldens may shift (the reducer now inlines what ir_inline did);
  re-bless and review diff carefully.

## Removes

Nothing yet — preparing for E.4.

comments.text: Open question: ir_inline::inline_module iterates to fixpoint (up to 32 rounds, capped by GROWTH_CAP). The reducer is single-pass. E.1 either introduces a fixpoint loop OR accepts single-pass inlining; if the latter, E.2's audit must measure the coverage loss. Default to single-pass and add loop only if E.2 surfaces real regressions.

Attempted implementation: added inline_stalled_callsites pass to reduce_module that iterates Stalled Direct callsites, mirrors ir_inline's predicates (is_inlinable / is_pure_tail_caller, skip closure_targets, skip boundary_fns, skip self-recursive), splices via alpha_rename + absorb_callee + Return→TailCall rewrite, writes CallsiteOutcome::Inlined, records Stalled for absorbed-body call-terminators to keep the .5 invariant green.

Findings — implementation is reverted, deeper integration work needed before .E can land:

5 tests broke under the parallel-operation interpretation. Two categories:

1. Reducer-isolation unit tests (does_not_reduce_call_with_opaque_arg, reducer_publishes_stalled_outcome_when_args_nonliteral) — expected. These test 'opaque arg → reducer leaves call alone' but the new pass inlines the small identity callee. Mechanical fix: use a non-inlinable callee (multi-block, or >INLINE_BUDGET stmts, or add to boundary_fns). Re-bless sanctioned by ticket.

2. Codegen correctness tests (hot_loop_inline_reduces_frame_allocs, condition_cache_bypasses_is_truthy_in_type_dispatch, pure_branch_type_test_emits_no_select) — NOT just goldens shift. All three panic at ir_codegen.rs:2064 with '.29.12.2: no live spec for closure target FnId(15)'. Root cause: the reducer runs AFTER ir_inline+ir_fuse in the codegen pipeline. ir_fuse may surface new inline opportunities ir_inline never saw (because ir_inline ran pre-fuse). The reducer then inlines a Direct callsite whose absorbed body or downstream effects remove the only spec covering a still-live MakeClosure'd lambda. ir_inline's careful early-pipeline ordering avoids this by running BEFORE specialisation/codegen-prep. The reducer adding inlining post-fuse breaks that ordering invariant.

Two paths forward, both larger than .11 as scoped:

A) Re-run ir_typer after reducer inlining (would catch the new MakeClosure-only any-key emit). Adds a typer pass; needs the typer's MakeClosure path to discover any-arity for fns referenced only by MakeClosure, even without a CallClosure site. Today the path is gated on opaque_arities_seen.

B) Restrict reducer inlining to callsites ir_inline would have found pre-fuse: snapshot module before ir_inline, replay predicates on the snapshot, only inline what ir_inline already inlined. Reframes 'reducer learns inline' as 'reducer mirrors ir_inline's *decisions*, not its *predicates*'. Cleaner outcome semantics; loses the 'reducer finds new opportunities ir_fuse exposed' upside (but that upside is precisely what broke codegen).

C) Have ir_inline itself write Inlined outcomes — the .11 spirit ('outcomes table records who decided what') with no behaviour change. Then .12 audit is trivial (ir_inline IS the inliner; outcomes table just records its work). .13/.14 absorb ir_inline into the reducer as a pre-fuse phase rather than a post-fuse one.

(C) is the elegant resolution and matches the epic's framing better. Filing as the next ticket once a clean break-down is on the table.

State: implementation reverted in this commit-set. Tree on .17 baseline. .11 left open for re-scope; .12-.16 transitively blocked.

Closed won't-pursue (not deferred). The 'reducer absorbs inline' framing is wrong: pipeline ordering is load-bearing. ir_inline runs pre-ir_fuse to preserve the typer invariant that MakeClosure'd lambdas with all-consumed CallClosure sites still get any-key spec emission. When the reducer (post-fuse) inlines a Direct callsite, ir_fuse may have surfaced opportunities ir_inline never saw, and the post-fuse inlining breaks the invariant (concrete failure: ir_codegen.rs:2064 panic '.29.12.2: no live spec for closure target FnId(15)' on hot_loop_inline_reduces_frame_allocs / condition_cache_bypasses_is_truthy_in_type_dispatch / pure_branch_type_test_emits_no_select). The consolidation that DID land in PR #18 (data model + block_callsites enumerator + reducer publishes outcomes + typer reads Stalled→Emitted) is what was tractable. The deeper unification — one fixpoint pass over fold+inline+emit — would be a much bigger redesign (GHC Simplifier shape) that the project doesn't currently need.

title: fz-CO.E.1 — Reducer learns inline criterion, writes Inlined outcome
